substances.name: Nitrous acid

substances.name: Methyl hydrogen sulfate

substances.notes: Corrosive alkyl sulfate chemistry; handle only with proper controls.

substances.uses: Green crystalline hydrate and iron(II) salt example.
substances.notes: Irritant; can be harmful if swallowed.

substances.name: Glauber's salt
substances.uses: Hydrated sodium sulfate reference and historic laxative salt.
substances.notes: May irritate; ingestion dose matters.

substances.name: Magnesium chloride hexahydrate
substances.uses: Hydrated salt example and source of magnesium ions.
substances.notes: May irritate eyes in concentrated form.

substances.name: Washing soda
substances.uses: Laundry/water-softening alkali and hydrate example.
substances.notes: Alkaline irritant.

substances.name: Zinc sulfate heptahydrate
substances.uses: Hydrated salt example and zinc salt in laboratory chemistry.
substances.notes: Irritant; harmful to aquatic life in quantity.

substances.name: Bromothymol blue
substances.uses: pH indicator near neutral range.
substances.notes: Dye/indicator; avoid ingestion.

substances.name: Litmus
substances.uses: Simple acid-base indicator: red in acid, blue in base.
substances.notes: Low hazard as paper strips; indicator solutions need normal lab care.

substances.name: Methyl orange
substances.uses: pH indicator useful around acidic endpoint titrations.
substances.notes: Dye/indicator; avoid dust ingestion.

substances.name: Methyl red
substances.uses: pH indicator for acidic transition range.
substances.notes: Dye/indicator; avoid dust exposure.

substances.name: Phenolphthalein
substances.uses: pH indicator: colorless in acid/neutral, pink in base.
substances.notes: Treat as lab chemical; avoid ingestion.

substances.name: Thymol blue
substances.uses: pH indicator with two transition ranges.
substances.notes: Dye/indicator; handle as reagent.

substances.name: Universal indicator
substances.uses: Shows approximate pH by color change over a broad acid-base range.
substances.notes: Treat as a chemical mixture; many solutions are alcohol-based.

substances.name: 2-Butanone
substances.uses: Solvent and simple ketone reference.
substances.notes: Highly flammable; vapor irritant.

substances.name: Acetone
substances.uses: Solvent, nail polish remover, lab cleaning.
substances.notes: Volatile and flammable.

substances.name: Acetophenone
substances.uses: Aromatic ketone used in fragrance and synthesis.
substances.notes: Irritant; harmful if swallowed.

substances.name: Benzophenone
substances.uses: UV absorber and aromatic ketone reference.
substances.notes: Irritant; environmental concerns for some uses.

substances.name: Camphor
substances.uses: Fragrant terpene ketone used in balms and moth repellents.
substances.notes: Flammable; harmful if swallowed in quantity.

substances.name: Cyclohexanone
substances.uses: Solvent and nylon precursor.
substances.notes: Flammable and harmful vapor.

substances.name: Air
substances.uses: Atmospheric gas mixture for combustion, respiration, and gas-law examples.
substances.notes: Compressed/oxygen-enriched air hazards depend on context.

substances.name: Baking powder
otherNames: Baking soda and tartaric acid mixture
substances.uses: Leavening agent for cakes and bread.
substances.notes: Produces carbon dioxide when heated/moistened, causing dough or batter to rise.

substances.name: Formalin
substances.uses: Laboratory preservation of dead animal bodies and biological specimens.
substances.notes: Aqueous formaldehyde solution; unsafe for food preservation.

substances.name: Rubbing alcohol
substances.uses: Antiseptic/cleaning alcohol solution.
substances.notes: Flammable; avoid inhalation and ignition sources.

substances.name: Seawater
substances.uses: Natural saltwater mixture and electrolyte reference.
substances.notes: Not potable without treatment.

substances.name: Tincture of iodine
substances.uses: Iodine antiseptic/stain and halogen solution example.
substances.notes: Irritant; stains; avoid ingestion.

substances.name: Vinegar
substances.uses: Food acid solution and household descaling/cleaning liquid.
substances.notes: Acidic irritant; never mix with bleach.

substances.name: Boron trifluoride
substances.uses: Lewis acid catalyst and boron halide reference.
substances.notes: Toxic corrosive gas.

substances.name: Carbon disulfide
substances.uses: Solvent and sulfur compound reference.
substances.notes: Highly flammable and toxic.

substances.name: Nitrogen trichloride
substances.uses: Reactive nitrogen chloride reference and pool-chemistry irritant.
substances.notes: Explosive/unstable in concentrated form.

substances.name: Phosphorus pentachloride
substances.uses: Chlorination reagent and decomposition example.
substances.notes: Reacts with water to release acidic fumes.

substances.name: Phosphorus trichloride
substances.uses: Product in phosphorus pentachloride decomposition and reagent in organophosphorus chemistry.
substances.notes: Toxic and water-reactive; releases acidic fumes.

substances.name: Silicon tetrachloride
substances.uses: Hydrolysis example and silicon chemistry precursor.
substances.notes: Fumes in moist air and forms hydrochloric acid.

substances.name: Sulfur hexafluoride
substances.uses: Electrical insulating gas and inert gas reference.
substances.notes: Asphyxiant; potent greenhouse gas.

substances.name: Water
substances.uses: Universal solvent, biological medium, coolant.
substances.notes: Neutral liquid at room temperature; expands on freezing.

substances.name: Xenon difluoride
substances.uses: Fluorinating agent and noble-gas compound example.
substances.notes: Strong oxidizer/fluorinating reagent.

substances.name: Adenine
substances.uses: Nucleobase in DNA/RNA and ATP.
substances.notes: Low hazard in small lab quantities; dust may irritate.

substances.name: Bauxite
substances.uses: Main mineral source of aluminium oxide for aluminium extraction.
substances.notes: Contains impurities such as iron oxide, titanium oxide and sand; treated with sodium hydroxide during purification.

substances.name: Calamine
substances.uses: Ore of zinc; converted to zinc oxide by calcination.
substances.notes: Heating releases carbon dioxide and leaves zinc oxide.

substances.name: Cassiterite
substances.uses: Main tin ore mineral.
substances.notes: Mineral dust hazard depends on processing.

substances.name: Chalcocite
substances.uses: Copper ore and possible component of copper corrosion layers.
substances.notes: Common copper(I) sulfide mineral; older spellings may vary.

substances.name: Chalcopyrite
substances.uses: Major ore of copper.
substances.notes: Sulfide ore that can produce sulfur dioxide during roasting.

substances.name: Chromite
substances.uses: Major chromium ore mineral.
substances.notes: Ore dust may contain hazardous chromium compounds.

substances.name: Cinnabar
substances.uses: Mercury sulfide ore mineral.
substances.notes: Mercury ore; toxic if processed or heated.

substances.name: Galena
substances.uses: Lead sulfide ore mineral.
substances.notes: Lead ore; toxic dust hazard.

substances.name: Hematite
substances.uses: Important ore of iron used in blast-furnace extraction.
substances.notes: Reduced by carbon monoxide to produce iron.

substances.name: Ilmenite
substances.uses: Iron titanium oxide ore for titanium.
substances.notes: Dust inhalation should be avoided.

substances.name: Iron pyrite
substances.uses: Iron sulfide mineral and source material in mineral chemistry.
substances.notes: A sulfide of iron mentioned as an iron mineral.

substances.name: Limonite
substances.uses: Hydrated iron ore.
substances.notes: Related to hydrated ferric oxide/rust compositions.

substances.name: Magnetite
substances.uses: Iron ore and magnetic mineral.
substances.notes: Can be concentrated by magnetic separation.

substances.name: Rutile
substances.uses: Titanium dioxide mineral and titanium ore.
substances.notes: Dust inhalation should be avoided.

substances.name: Zinc blende
substances.uses: Important ore of zinc.
substances.notes: Roasting converts zinc sulfide to zinc oxide before reduction.

substances.name: Aluminum oxide
substances.uses: Abrasives, ceramics, aluminum production.
substances.notes: Hard amphoteric oxide.

substances.name: Calcium oxide
substances.uses: Cement, steelmaking, drying agent.
substances.notes: Reacts exothermically with water.

substances.name: Carbon dioxide
substances.uses: Carbonated drinks, fire extinguishers, photosynthesis reactant.
substances.notes: Can displace oxygen in confined spaces.

substances.name: Carbon monoxide
substances.uses: Combustion product and reducing gas.
substances.notes: Extremely toxic; binds hemoglobin.

substances.name: Hydrated ferric oxide
substances.uses: Corrosion product of iron.
substances.notes: Forms slowly from iron in the presence of oxygen and water.

substances.name: Iron(III) oxide
substances.uses: Pigments, iron ore.
substances.notes: Red-brown solid; common corrosion product.

substances.name: Manganese dioxide
substances.uses: Dry-cell depolarizer/cathode material and catalyst in oxygen preparation.
substances.notes: Dust can irritate lungs; avoid inhalation.

substances.name: Manganese(III) oxide
substances.uses: Product shown in simplified dry-cell discharge reactions.
substances.notes: Dust may irritate the respiratory tract.

substances.name: Nitric oxide
substances.uses: High-temperature nitrogen oxidation product and smog precursor.
substances.notes: Toxic gas; oxidizes in air to nitrogen dioxide.

substances.name: Nitrogen dioxide
substances.uses: Air pollutant and precursor to nitric acid in acid rain.
substances.notes: Toxic reddish-brown gas; severe respiratory irritant.

substances.name: Nitrous oxide
substances.uses: Anesthetic gas and nitrogen oxide reference.
substances.notes: Asphyxiant/oxidizer; misuse is dangerous.

substances.name: Phosphorus pentoxide
substances.uses: Dehydrating agent and acidic oxide reference.
substances.notes: Reacts strongly with water; corrosive.

substances.name: Silicon dioxide
substances.uses: Quartz/sand/glass component and network oxide.
substances.notes: Respirable crystalline silica dust is hazardous.

substances.name: Sulfur dioxide
substances.uses: Intermediate in sulfuric acid manufacture; produced by burning sulfur.
substances.notes: Toxic acidic gas; forms sulfurous acid in rainwater.

substances.name: Sulfur trioxide
substances.uses: Sulfuric acid precursor and acid-rain intermediate.
substances.notes: Fuming corrosive oxide.

substances.name: Titanium dioxide
substances.uses: White pigment in paints, coatings, and sunscreens.
substances.notes: Dust inhalation concerns; product context matters.

substances.name: Vanadium(V) oxide
substances.uses: Catalyst in the contact process for sulfuric acid.
substances.notes: Used around 450-500 C for oxidation of sulfur dioxide.

substances.name: Zinc oxide
substances.uses: Sunscreen, ointments, rubber, and pigment uses.
substances.notes: Dust may irritate; aquatic hazard in some forms.

substances.name: Barium peroxide
substances.uses: Oxidizer and historic oxygen/peroxide chemistry reagent.
substances.notes: Toxic barium oxidizer.

substances.name: Benzoyl peroxide
substances.uses: Acne treatment and polymerization initiator.
substances.notes: Oxidizer; can bleach fabrics and irritate skin.

substances.name: Calcium peroxide
substances.uses: Oxygen-release compound and peroxide reference.
substances.notes: Oxidizer; irritant.

substances.name: Hydrogen peroxide
substances.uses: Disinfectant, bleaching, oxidation.
substances.notes: Decomposes to water and oxygen.

substances.name: Sodium peroxide
substances.uses: Oxidizer and peroxide salt reference.
substances.notes: Strong oxidizer; reacts with water/acids.

substances.name: Aviation kerosene
substances.uses: Jet-fuel kerosene fraction.
substances.notes: Flammable combustible fuel; vapor hazard.

substances.name: Bitumen
substances.uses: Road construction and waterproofing.
substances.notes: High-boiling residue from petroleum distillation.

substances.name: Diesel oil
substances.uses: Vehicle fuel, lubricant and solvent applications.
substances.notes: Heavier petroleum fraction than kerosene.

substances.name: Gasoline
substances.uses: Fuel for vehicle engines.
substances.notes: Petroleum fraction with a low boiling range, about 21-70 C in common school-level tables.

substances.name: Heavy fuel oil
substances.uses: Residual fuel for ships, boilers, and industrial heating.
substances.notes: Combustible and pollutant-rich; handling requires controls.

substances.name: Kerosene
substances.uses: Jet fuel, lamp fuel and heating fuel.
substances.notes: Middle petroleum fraction.

substances.name: Liquefied petroleum gas
substances.uses: Cooking fuel and heating fuel.
substances.notes: Mainly propane and butane stored as a liquid under pressure.

substances.name: Lubricating oil
substances.uses: Heavy petroleum fraction used to reduce friction in engines and machinery.
substances.notes: Combustible; used oil may contain hazardous contaminants.

substances.name: Mineral spirits
substances.uses: Paint thinner/solvent petroleum fraction.
substances.notes: Flammable; vapor irritant.

substances.name: Naphtha
substances.uses: Fuel and petrochemical feedstock.
substances.notes: Fraction used to produce chemical substances in petrochemical industries.

substances.name: Paraffin wax
substances.uses: Toiletries, candles, coatings and petroleum jelly products.
substances.notes: Solid petroleum fraction with long-chain saturated hydrocarbons.

substances.name: Petroleum coke
substances.uses: Carbon-rich refinery solid used as fuel or industrial carbon source.
substances.notes: Dust can irritate and be combustible.

substances.name: Petroleum gas
substances.uses: Fuel gas and feedstock; can be liquefied under pressure.
substances.notes: Lowest boiling petroleum fraction, roughly 0-20 C.

substances.name: Phenol
substances.uses: Phenolic compound used in resins, disinfectant history, and aromatic chemistry reference.
substances.notes: Not an alcohol: its OH group is bonded directly to an aromatic ring.

substances.name: Bakelite
substances.uses: Phenol-formaldehyde thermoset used in older handles and electrical parts.
substances.notes: Dust from machining can irritate.

substances.name: Delrin
substances.uses: Chairs, tables, buckets and molded plastic objects.
substances.notes: Polymer made from formaldehyde/methanal units.

substances.name: Natural rubber
substances.uses: Elastic material; vulcanized with sulfur for durability.
substances.notes: Natural polymer obtained from rubber tree latex.

substances.name: Neoprene
substances.uses: Synthetic rubber used in wetsuits, gloves, and gaskets.
substances.notes: Burning/degradation fumes hazardous.

substances.name: Nylon 6,6
substances.uses: Artificial cloth, rope and toothbrush bristles.
substances.notes: Condensation polymer from adipic acid and hexamethylenediamine.

substances.name: Polycarbonate
substances.uses: Transparent engineering plastic for lenses, discs, and housings.
substances.notes: Thermal degradation and additives vary.

substances.name: Polyethylene
substances.uses: Plastic bags, bottles, films, containers. Plastic bags, sheets and durable plastic items.
substances.notes: Polymer of ethene. Addition polymer made from ethene.

substances.name: Polyethylene terephthalate
substances.uses: PET bottles, fibers, and films.
substances.notes: Stable plastic; recycling/heat context matters.

substances.name: Polymethyl methacrylate
substances.uses: Acrylic glass/Plexiglas material.
substances.notes: Combustible; machining dust can irritate.

substances.name: Polypropylene
substances.uses: Ropes, pipes, carpets, bottles and heat-resistant plastic goods.
substances.notes: Addition polymer from propene.

substances.name: Polystyrene
substances.uses: Foam/rigid plastic for packaging, insulation, and labware.
substances.notes: Combustible plastic; monomer/residue context matters.

substances.name: Polytetrafluoroethylene
substances.uses: Nonstick coatings, gaskets, chemical-resistant parts.
substances.notes: Very low surface energy fluoropolymer.

substances.name: Polyvinyl chloride
substances.uses: Pipes, cable insulation, flooring. Water pipes, wire insulation and medical devices.
substances.notes: Chlorinated polymer; additives affect properties. Addition polymer made from vinyl chloride.

substances.name: Urea-formaldehyde resin
substances.uses: Household appliances such as plates, mugs and molded items.
substances.notes: Condensation polymer made from urea and formaldehyde.

substances.name: Ammonium carbamate
substances.uses: Intermediate in industrial urea production.
substances.notes: Forms from ammonia and carbon dioxide under pressure.

substances.name: Ammonium chloride
substances.uses: Laboratory preparation of ammonia and byproduct in baking soda preparation.
substances.notes: Heating with calcium oxide or calcium hydroxide releases ammonia.

substances.name: Ammonium cyanate
substances.uses: Historical example of conversion to urea.
substances.notes: Handle as lab chemical; avoid ingestion and dust.

substances.name: Ammonium hydrogen carbonate
substances.uses: Intermediate in Solvay-style baking soda preparation.
substances.notes: Forms from carbon dioxide, ammonia and water.

substances.name: Ammonium nitrate
substances.uses: Fertilizer, cold packs.
substances.notes: Strong oxidizer; controlled due to explosion risk.

substances.name: Ammonium sulfate
substances.uses: Fertilizer and ammonium salt reference.
substances.notes: Dust may irritate.

substances.name: Calcium carbide
substances.uses: Preparation of acetylene/ethyne gas.
substances.notes: Reacts with water to form ethyne and calcium hydroxide.

substances.name: Calcium carbonate
substances.uses: Antacid, cement, filler, shells.
substances.notes: Reacts with acids to release carbon dioxide.

substances.name: Calcium chloride
substances.uses: Road de-icing, drying agent, brines.
substances.notes: Hygroscopic; dissolving releases heat.

substances.name: Calcium phosphate
substances.uses: Bone/mineral chemistry and fertilizer reference.
substances.notes: Low hazard as mineral; dust may irritate.

substances.name: Calcium silicate
substances.uses: Forms as molten slag when calcium oxide reacts with silicon dioxide in iron extraction.
substances.notes: Separates gangue from molten iron in the blast furnace.

substances.name: Calcium stearate
substances.uses: Forms scum when soap reacts with calcium ions in hard water.
substances.notes: Low hazard solid; dust may irritate.

substances.name: Calcium sulfate
substances.uses: Drywall, plaster, cement.
substances.notes: Hydrates form gypsum/plaster materials.

substances.name: Copper(II) nitrate
substances.uses: Copper salt used in precipitation/redox examples.
substances.notes: Oxidizer and irritant; harmful if swallowed.

substances.name: Copper(II) sulfate
substances.uses: Electroplating, fungicide, lab reagent.
substances.notes: Blue hydrate crystals; toxic to aquatic life.

substances.name: Cryolite
substances.uses: Lowers the melting point of aluminium oxide during aluminium extraction.
substances.notes: Molten aluminium oxide with cryolite conducts electricity at lower temperature.

substances.name: Ferric chloride
substances.uses: Oxidizing chloride salt used in tests, etching, and water-treatment chemistry.
substances.notes: Corrosive; stains and can irritate skin and eyes.

substances.name: Ferrous chloride
substances.uses: Iron(II) salt used in redox examples and as a precursor to iron(III) compounds.
substances.notes: Irritant; handle as a laboratory salt.

substances.name: Lead(II) iodide
substances.uses: Bright yellow precipitate in lead nitrate and potassium iodide tests.
substances.notes: Lead compound; toxic and cumulative.

substances.name: Lead(II) nitrate
substances.uses: Soluble lead salt used in precipitation examples.
substances.notes: Toxic oxidizer; lead exposure hazard.

substances.name: Magnesium sulfate
substances.uses: Bath salts, magnesium supplement, drying agent.
substances.notes: Often encountered as heptahydrate.

substances.name: Monosodium glutamate
substances.uses: Umami flavor enhancer.
substances.notes: Sodium salt of glutamic acid.

substances.name: Potassium carbonate
substances.uses: Soap/glass making, alkalizing agent.
substances.notes: Basic carbonate salt.

substances.name: Potassium dichromate
substances.uses: Oxidizing chromium salt used in classic redox chemistry.
substances.notes: Toxic carcinogenic hexavalent chromium compound.

substances.name: Potassium iodide
substances.uses: Iodine source, thyroid protection in emergencies.
substances.notes: Iodide can be oxidized to iodine.

substances.name: Potassium nitrate
substances.uses: Fertilizer, food curing, fireworks oxidizer.
substances.notes: Oxidizing nitrate salt.

substances.name: Potassium permanganate
substances.uses: Oxidizer, disinfectant chemistry, and redox titration reagent.
substances.notes: Strong oxidizer; stains and reacts with organics.

substances.name: Silver nitrate
substances.uses: Chloride test, silvering, antiseptic use.
substances.notes: Light-sensitive; stains skin.

substances.name: Sodium acetate
substances.uses: Buffer component, hot-ice packs, and acetate salt reference.
substances.notes: Low hazard; concentrated solutions may irritate.

substances.name: Sodium aluminate
substances.uses: Intermediate in purification of bauxite and aluminium oxide preparation.
substances.notes: Formed when hydrated aluminium oxide reacts with sodium hydroxide.

substances.name: Sodium benzoate
substances.uses: Food preservative in acidic foods/drinks.
substances.notes: Benzoate salt of benzoic acid.

substances.name: Sodium bicarbonate
substances.uses: Baking, antacid, mild cleaning. Leavening agent and antacid-like household chemical.
substances.notes: Releases CO2 with acids or heat.

substances.name: Sodium borate
substances.uses: Laundry booster, slime recipes, cleaning.
substances.notes: Keep away from children; not food-safe.

substances.name: Sodium carbonate
substances.uses: Glass making, detergents, water softening. Cleaning material and precursor to washing soda.
substances.notes: Basic salt; forms alkaline solutions.

substances.name: Sodium chloride
substances.uses: Food seasoning, saline solutions, de-icing. Seasoning, saline preparation, food preservation and raw material for sodium hydroxide.
substances.notes: High melting ionic solid; soluble in water.

substances.name: Sodium citrate
substances.uses: Buffering salt, cheese emulsifier, anticoagulant tubes.
substances.notes: Salt of citric acid.

substances.name: Sodium fluoride
substances.uses: Toothpaste and water fluoridation.
substances.notes: Toxic in high doses.

substances.name: Sodium nitrate
substances.uses: Fertilizer, oxidizer, and nitrate salt reference.
substances.notes: Oxidizer; ingestion hazard in quantity.

substances.name: Sodium silicate
substances.uses: Component/related material in glass chemistry and cleaners.
substances.notes: Glass cleaners are chosen so they do not attack silicate glass.

substances.name: Sodium stearate
substances.uses: Main component of many soaps.
substances.notes: Sodium salt of stearic acid.

substances.name: Sodium sulfate
substances.uses: Detergents, paper pulping, drying agent.
substances.notes: Stable, water-soluble salt.

substances.name: Sodium tartrate
substances.uses: Product when baking soda reacts with tartaric acid in baking powder.
substances.notes: Carbon dioxide from the same reaction causes cakes to rise.

substances.name: Sodium thiosulfate
substances.uses: Iodine titrations, photographic fixer, dechlorination.
substances.notes: Reduces iodine to iodide.

substances.name: Trisodium phosphate
substances.uses: Cleaner/building salt and phosphate reference.
substances.notes: Alkaline irritant.

substances.name: Zinc chloride
substances.uses: Electrolyte component in dry cells and chloride salt examples.
substances.notes: Corrosive and harmful if swallowed.

substances.name: Quaternary ammonium compounds
substances.uses: Cationic disinfectant/surfactant family used in many cleaners.
substances.notes: A broad family of cationic surfactants/disinfectants rather than one fixed molecule.

substances.name: Sodium lauryl sulfate
substances.uses: Surfactant in detergents, toothpaste, and shampoos.
substances.notes: An anionic surfactant salt, not a simple inorganic salt.
